other_observers: Brian Gibbons, Rachel Hopper; First found by Gary Matthews on 31 August 03
specific_location: On the north edge of Crow Valley Campground (Pawnee NG) between the single campsites in the NW corner and the group campsite in the NE corner
description: An obvious 'Solitary' Vireo (white spectacles on dark head, white throat, white wing bars), the bird had a strongly blue-gray head, except for a bright white throat that contrasted strongly and sharply with the blue-gray auriculars. The head also contrasted fairly strongly with the bright green back and rump. The underparts were white except for bright greenish-yellow flanks and vent strap.  The bill was slightly two-toned with a blackish culmen and a dark blue-gray to the base of the mandible. The eyes were dark, the legs were blue-gray.  The wings were black with two whitish wing bars and each remex was edged with white (outer primaries and tertials) or pale yellow (secondaries).Behaviors: Foraging fairly lethargically in cottonwoodCall: none heardPlumage: Basic
